name: Charlie Shavers
abstract: Charles James Shavers (August 3, 1920 – July 8, 1971) was an American jazz trumpeter who played with Dizzy Gillespie, Nat King Cole, Roy Eldridge, Johnny Dodds, Jimmie Noone, Sidney Bechet, Midge Williams, Tommy Dorsey, and Billie Holiday. He was also an arranger and composer, and one of his compositions, "Undecided", is a jazz standard.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: We were roommates for three years in Tommy Dorsey’s band. ... I learned a lot rooming with Charlie. He taught me a lot about music
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Shavers was in Tommy’s band for three years too. We were roommates.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: In that band, Raymond Scott and Webster had black and white -- they had Charlie Shavers playing trumpet... During this period of time, Charlie Shavers who apparently -- we had just worked together for the first time

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: the very first tune was a blues thing that Charlie Shavers had written... he did this kind of a test with all the guys that played solo in the band, like DeFranco and Charlie Shavers and Ziggy and all.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: he had Charlie Shavers... Well we had a good first trumpet player in there called Carl Warrick... And Charlie Shavers….fantastic trumpet player... Charlie Shavers took most of the solos
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: So, a couple times I got a chance to play with a couple of really great jazz legends like Arvell Shaw… with Pop’s. Charlie Shavers; which was fantastic.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
